response: The `debounce` function returns a decorator that, when applied to a function `fn`, creates a wrapper (`debounced`) which, on each call, cancels any pending `threading.Timer` and starts a new one that will invoke `fn` after `wait` seconds; thus `fn` executes only after the caller has stopped invoking the wrapped function for at least `wait` seconds. This implements the debounce pattern using a closure‑held timer object.
